授業目的: The main purpose of this course is to improve and develop students' communication and confidence to use English in personal, professional, and academic contexts. Although the course will focus primarily on spoken communication, lessons will also aim at developing listening, reading, and writing skills to achieve and reinforce communicative goals. Lesson goals and tasks will be centered around a wide variety of skills topics relevant to student interests. Students will be encouraged to develop their intercultural awareness by critically thinking about and expressing their ideas from personal, global, and cultural perspectives. In-class communication and other activities will aim at improving students' capacity to work collaboratively and in cooperation with classmates. Students will be asked to reflect on their difficulties regarding English communication, describe how they plan to overcome these difficulties, and reflect on their progress by the end of the semester.
到達目標: Students will improve and build their confidence to use English communicatively in a variety of situations and contexts. Students will improve their spoken English fluency: improving speaking, listening, reading, and writing needed for effective communication in a variety of situations. Students will improve their lexical range by learning and using vocabulary skills related to course content. Students will improve their critical thinking skills and will improve their intercultural awareness by exploring and sharing ideas from personal, global, and cultural perspectives. Students will reflect on their personal progress throughout the course.
授業方法: Course will be aimed at a face to face format, but methods of studying online will also be provided. Students will be required to do in-class activities individually and collaboratively, and complete homework as required.

学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic:
Each course is placed within the curriculum based on the Diploma Policy (DP) and Curriculum Policy (CP) established by each faculty and graduate school. 
By studying these courses with the DP and CP in mind, you will develop the abilities expected by each faculty and graduate school. 
The DP, CP, and curriculum schedule (course list, etc.) for each faculty and graduate school are available at the link below; please use them as a reference for your study plan. 
https://kwic.kwansei.ac.jp/cabinet/reference?typeCd=0&cabinetId=557&directLink=1 
*This is a link to kwic (accessible only to current students).

備考Note0:
Prerequisite: Taking the TOEIC test required by KGU in December is a prerequisite for earning credit.    
Absences: Four or more absences are considered excessive and result in automatic failure. Leaving a classroom without asking for permission, doing something other than class activities, or sleeping is considered an absence. 
Tardiness: Arriving in the classroom after the bell is considered tardiness. Arriving 30 minutes after the bell is considered an absence. Two tardy arrivals equal one absence. (Being late is not considered tardiness in the case of     
documented delay of public transportation.) Make-up Assessments: Make-up assessments will not be given except in cases of documented medical necessity or family emergency. Cheating: 
Cheating in any quizzes, tests, projects, 
or assignments 
results in zero points on the work. Please note that leaving the classroom without permission from the teacher, working on other subjects, or sleeping during class will be counted as an absence. 
Lateness: Entering the classroom after the bell rings will be considered lateness. 30 minutes or more late will be considered an absence. Also, two instances of lateness will be counted as one absence. (Delays due to public transportation will not be counted as lateness.) 
Alternative exams and assignments: Alternative exams and assignments will only be permitted if there is a notification of school suspension or the death of a family member. 
Cheating: If cheating is found in any assignment, including quizzes, exams, submissions, and homework, the score for that assignment assignment will be zero.
